rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL GIEDGOWD VS. KEVIN MCGUINNESS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 5, 2012, Line 1. DEFENDANT KEVIN MCGUINNESS' Motion To Tax Costs. Granted as to the claimed costs in items 5 and 8 and denied as to all other costs that have been challenged. As to item 5, there is no showing that counsel was served with process. The lack of merit to item 8 is conceded. All other costs are proper. A total of $519 is stricken. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT VS. JAMILEH K MSALAM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions; Memo Of P & A; Dec. And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for Hearing on Thursday, April 5, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT WESTERN COLLECTION BUREAU'S Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions - Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Sanctions imposed in the amount of $340. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to TSchoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANNA FLIDER VS. JOSEPH DUGAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 5, 2012, Line 5. PLAINTIFF ANNA FLIDER'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees. Denied. Per Paragraph 17A of the contract, Plaintiff forfeited the right to seek fees because she pursued litigation prior to mediation. The tort of another doctrine does not apply to this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CARL JONES et al VS. VICTORIANO COSTANEDA-GOYTIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Production Of Real Property & For Sanctions; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for Hearing on Thursday, April 5, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFFs CARL JONES and RONALD JONES' Motion To Compel Production Of Real Property & For Sanctions - Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied. The parties agree that the plaintiff's request for inspection implicates a right to privacy. Plaintiff must meet a higher burden for obtaining discovery that implicates a privacy right. Plaintiff has not met this burden. Plaintiff, who did not file a reply brief, has not made a showing that the request for inspection is directly relevant to a cause of action or defense or that it is essential to a fair resolution of the lawsuit, nor has he established that the information he seeks to discover (which concerns the amount of time the defendants spend in their Fairfield house) cannot be obtained through less intrusive means. See, e.g., Vinson v. Superior Court, 43 Cal.3d 833, 8430844 (1987). Given that the defendants admittedly still occupy the Fairfield house several days per week, the Pro Tem judge is persuaded by the defendants' argument that an inspection is not likely to yield evidence bearing on the question of frequency of use of the Fairfield house, which evidence can be obtained through less intrusive means. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to TSchoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
